title: Dockerized two-machine MQTT validation (no UDS path)
description: Validate cross-machine realtime semantics by forcing MQTT-only transport in isolated Docker containers (no shared UDS), exercising publish/subscribe and Kanbus envelope flow against AWS IoT custom-authorizer path.
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Started Docker-isolated multi-machine MQTT validation design to avoid UDS transport masking.
Evidence: task activated with explicit scope against AWS IoT custom-authorizer path.
Next: run two isolated MQTT clients in separate containers (subscriber + publisher), verify message delivery on tenant topic, and capture logs.
Risk/Blocker: none.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Ran container-isolated MQTT test harness (two separate Docker clients) against AWS IoT custom-authorizer path.
Evidence: both subscriber and publisher containers reached CONNECT over WSS; subscriber reached SUB_READY; publish command returned PUB_SENT.
Next: investigate missing subscriber delivery for token-based publish path (likely policy/authorizer behavior) and add definitive pass/fail gate.
Risk/Blocker: message delivery from API-token publisher to API-token subscriber not yet observed despite successful connect/subscribe.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Ran real kbs->kbs cross-repo cloud MQTT mutation probes (forced transport=mqtt, no UDS) and captured deterministic failure mode.
Evidence: both watcher and publisher hit connection reset/timeouts even when authorizer logs show api_token allow; kbs create emits realtime publish failed and watcher sees reset.
Next: isolate post-auth failure in token MQTT path (subscribe/publish authorization or rumqttc transport behavior) and patch kbs cloud topic/runtime config accordingly.
Risk/Blocker: cross-machine kbs mutation over API-token MQTT is not yet proven; webapp/browser MQTT path remains green.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Re-ran deterministic 2-workspace cloud MQTT mutation probe after restart. Evidence: create_rc=0, watch_matches=0, watcher stderr="I/O: Connection reset by peer (os error 54)" while using token scopes subscribe,publish on projects/anthus/kanbus/events. Next: capture IoT connect/sub logs + authorizer CloudWatch for this exact token/session and isolate reset cause in kbs MQTT path. Risk/Blocker: kbs->kbs mutation propagation over cloud MQTT remains blocked despite successful token issuance/auth path setup.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Identified and fixed cloud MQTT token-path connectivity root cause in kbs (custom-authorizer ALPN). Evidence: mqtt token connect succeeds with ALPN=mqtt; kbs watcher now shows ConnAck/SubAck and receives issue.mutated envelope in forced-MQTT two-workspace probe. Next: harden publish flush semantics and extend deterministic validation window/artifacts for consistent pass. Risk/Blocker: short observation windows can miss late delivery; validation harness timing must be tightened before closing.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Re-validated forced-MQTT two-workspace mutation flow after ALPN fix and publish-loop hardening. Evidence: latest probe produced watch_matches=1 with received issue.mutated envelope and create command completed without realtime warnings. Next: fold this into scripted pre-acceptance gate artifacts and re-run full cloud validation sequence from auth/token gates onward. Risk/Blocker: issue state remains blocked by dependency constraints in tracker; technical MQTT mutation path is now demonstrably working in this probe.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Set project-level AWS MQTT defaults in .kanbus.override.yml for live dogfood testing. Evidence: configured transport=mqtt, IoT broker endpoint, custom authorizer, API token, and topic projects/anthus/kanbus/events; default kbs mutation executed without transport flags. Next: keep realtime soak testing and monitor clock/kbsc behavior under restart scenarios. Risk/Blocker: occasional mqtt connect timeout observed intermittently during long run.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Re-ran 10-event MQTT test after setting project default realtime config to AWS IoT in .kanbus.override.yml. Evidence: issue tskl-eo2.11 received timed create/update/comment/label/close mutations; default watcher (no transport flags) captured issue.mutated envelopes for this issue from 13:20:20Z through 13:21:23Z. Next: continue soak runs and compare watcher continuity across clock restarts to explain perceived missing intermediate events. Risk/Blocker: watcher stream can miss intermediate mutations during reconnect windows; envelopes are latest-snapshot, not guaranteed per-mutation replay.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Corrected realtime config target from repo-level ~/Projects/Kanbus to master board root ~/Projects as requested. Evidence: wrote ~/Projects/.kanbus.override.yml with AWS IoT broker + custom authorizer + API token + projects/anthus/kanbus/events topic; default watcher in ~/Projects captured issue.mutated envelopes for new root issue. Next: restart clock against ~/Projects and rerun 10-event sequence for observer continuity validation. Risk/Blocker: none.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Re-ran full 10-event root-board test after user-confirmed clock restart, from ~/Projects with default MQTT config. Evidence: new root issue kanbus-f5de69 received create/update/comment/label/close sequence 13:24:38Z..13:26:40Z; watcher log /tmp/projects_root_mqtt_rerun_20260308T132433Z_watch.log captured issue.mutated envelopes for that issue. Next: compare clock-observed transitions against this run tag and continue soak if needed. Risk/Blocker: intermittent single publish timeouts remain possible; latest run completed all scripted events.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Progress: Validated VM-style MQTT then Git convergence using Docker-isolated watcher and separate host/vm clones. Evidence: vm base stayed open while MQTT overlay advanced to blocked; reconcile before pull retained overrides; after git pull, reconcile removed overlay entry (removed=1, pruned=5, overlay_exists_after_pull_reconcile=false). Next: integrate this flow into scripted pre-acceptance validation artifact step. Risk/Blocker: none.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Fixed local console snapshot 500s caused by cloud MQTT override fields not recognized by Python config schema.
Evidence: commit 03399e7; local checks passed (conda snapshot OK, /api/config=200, /api/issues=200, rust clippy locked clean).
Next: monitor CI run for 03399e7 and continue pre-acceptance gate validation.
Risk/Blocker: none for this defect; broader gate completion still pending.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: CI loop resumed; latest dev run failed at coverage-ratchet after clippy was fixed.
Evidence: GitHub Actions run 22824494521 failed only in coverage-ratchet step.
Next: compute exact coverage delta from run artifacts and add targeted tests to recover threshold.
Risk/Blocker: merge velocity blocked until baseline gate is green.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Added targeted Python tests for issue delete + console snapshot paths; local ratchet now passes.
Evidence: coverage_ratchet passed with python_line_coverage=77.66 (baseline=77.46).
Next: Commit/push coverage fix and monitor latest dev CI run in timed loop.
Risk/Blocker: None currently.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Pushed coverage recovery commit to dev and triggered a new CI run.
Evidence: commit cea2d8f (test(py): recover coverage ratchet with snapshot and delete tests).
Next: Poll GitHub Actions every 5 minutes until latest CI run is success.
Risk/Blocker: Pending remote run results.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: CI run 22825200266 failed in beads-interop after coverage passed; root cause is delete confirmation enforcement.
Evidence: failed step called --beads delete without --yes and returned 'delete requires confirmation'.
Next: Patched tools/run_beads_interop_suite.py to add --yes, then re-push and re-monitor CI.
Risk/Blocker: Local full interop rerun is limited by disk pressure; relying on CI verification for this fix.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: CI is green on dev after coverage recovery + beads interop delete confirmation fix.
Evidence: GitHub Actions CI run 22825736642 conclusion=success (all jobs passed, including beads-interop and coverage-ratchet).
Next: Continue cloud pre-acceptance validation workstream from current task queue.
Risk/Blocker: Local machine disk pressure can limit heavyweight local Beads builds; CI remains source of truth.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:

Evidence: latest dev run is in progress (CI 22825889119, head: 'Implement wiki links and configurable path').
Next: poll every 600s and report only state transitions/failures.
Risk/Blocker: none.

Evidence: CI run 22825889119 conclusion=success.
